Cooperstown Arcade

Two ways to play. Real career stats, every time.
☕ Buy Me a Coffee

🏟️ The Hall of Fame Game

Draft a 15-man roster of real Hall of Famers, set your lineup and pitching staff, then play exhibitions or a full 162-game season against CPU clubs.

⚔️ Cooperstown Clash

No drafting — pick any two World Series championship teams from 1927–2022 and settle it head to head. 1927 Yankees vs. 1998 Yankees, 1975 Reds vs. 2016 Cubs, whatever you want.

Pick Two Championship Teams

Each roster is auto-filled from that team's actual real-life season — best real starter at each position, real starting pitcher, real bench and bullpen. No drafting.

VS

🕹️ Reigning Champ

The team-year with the most wins across everyone's battles. Think you can beat it?

Auto-filled from each team's real season — best player at each position, real starter and bullpen. Reorder batting or swap who starts.

Team A

Pitching Staff

Team B

Pitching Staff

Set your team name, then start the draft. You'll draft 8 field starters, a designated hitter, a starting pitcher, 3 bench bats, and 2 relievers (15 rounds) in a snake draft against a CPU GM.

Set Your Batting Order

Drag isn't needed — use the arrows. Your pitcher always bats 9th.

Set Your Pitching Staff

Whichever pitcher is drafted first defaults to starter — that's not always right (a closer like Rivera has almost no starts). Pick who actually starts; the other two become your bullpen.

Exhibition: one game vs. your draft rival. Season: a real 162-game season across a 6-team league (you, your rival, and 4 auto-built clubs from the remaining pool), then the top two by record meet in a championship. Note: pitching staffs are 3 arms, not a real 5-man rotation — a season cycles your 3 drafted pitchers through the starts rather than running one guy out there 162 times. It's a simulation, not a full roster sim.

🕹️ Arcade Leaderboard — shared and public. Every season anyone completes here shows up for everyone. Think you can top the board?

Career stats sourced from the Chadwick Baseball Bureau / Sean Lahman Baseball Database (CC BY-SA), covering BBWAA & Veterans Committee inductees. Team shown is the franchise each player logged the most career games with. OVR is a 20–80 scouting-scale grade against the full HOF pool. Value (VBD) compares a player to the best remaining talent at their position. Pitcher fatigue is a house-rule stamina model based on batters faced, not an official metric. Single d20 card per plate appearance.
